scenario: Yarrow SaaS runs a Camunda 8.8 tenant provisioning workflow handling 16000 tenants per month. A call activity sits inside a parallel multi-instance branch, and the architect wants to prevent variables produced by sibling branches from accidentally overriding each other when the child process instances complete.
question: Which configuration on the call activity reduces the risk of accidental variable overrides when it sits inside a parallel flow?

options.text: Set propagateAllParentVariables to false on the call activity so no variables from the parent process are copied into the child process instance on activation by Zeebe
Leave propagateAllChildVariables at its default value of true on the call activity so all child variables are merged into the parent process scope on completion of the child
Disable propagateAllChildVariables or define output mappings on the call activity so only the variables listed in those mappings propagate from the child to the parent scope
Set propagateAllChildVariables to true and add input mappings on the call activity so that every child variable is renamed by Zeebe before propagation to the parent scope

a.text: Incorrect. propagateAllParentVariables controls whether parent variables flow into the child; the accidental override risk is on the child-to-parent direction, not the parent-to-child direction.

b.text: Incorrect. Leaving propagateAllChildVariables at its true default is exactly the configuration that produces accidental overrides in parallel branches, so it does not reduce the risk.

c.text: Correct. The documentation recommends disabling propagateAllChildVariables or defining output mappings on the call activity in a parallel flow, so only intentionally listed variables propagate back to the parent scope.

d.text: Incorrect. Input mappings affect what is copied into the child process instance, not what is propagated back to the parent, so they do not address the override risk.

explanation: When a call activity sits in a parallel flow, the documentation recommends disabling propagateAllChildVariables or defining output mappings so only intentionally selected variables propagate from the child instance to the parent scope.
